findings.description: reachable() has no visited-guard: it enqueues every neighbor unconditionally, so on any cyclic graph (the exact case its docstring promises to support) the queue never drains, causing an infinite loop and unbounded memory growth.

reachable() never adds `start` to `seen` (only neighbor nodes are added), so an isolated start node yields an empty set and `start` is inconsistently excluded from its own reachable set unless a self-loop exists.

Reading `self._edges[node]` on a defaultdict(list) (here and at lines 40, 54, 61) inserts an empty list for every leaf node touched, silently mutating `_edges` and polluting the key set that topological_order() later iterates at line 46.

count_paths() recurses with no cycle guard despite operating on the same graph type reachable() claims may be cyclic; a cyclic input causes unbounded recursion (RecursionError), and deep DAGs can also overflow the recursion limit (exponential cost without memoization is a related performance concern — deferred to critic-performance).
count_paths() returns 0 indistinguishably for 'no path exists', 'dst unreachable', and 'src/dst not in graph', giving callers no way to detect an invalid/absent node argument.
